name: Seated Machine Back Extension
resistance_profile: A pad against the upper back provides even, cam-based resistance as the torso extends; the pelvis is strapped to isolate the lumbar erectors.
cues: Drive back with the low back, not the head
Extend to just past upright — don't slam into hyperextension
Control the return into the stretch
execution_steps: Sit in the machine with the pad across the shoulder blades and the pelvis secured by the thigh or lap restraint.
Begin flexed forward at the target start angle, gripping the handles.
Extend the torso backward by driving through the low back until just past upright.
Pause briefly, then return under control to the flexed start without letting the stack slam.
good_when: You want isolated, low-skill lumbar erector work
You want to add erector volume without loading the entire posterior chain
bad_when: The machine's fixed start angle forces painful end-range flexion or extension
You jerk the pad instead of moving smoothly
common_errors: Using momentum to fling the pad backward
progressions: Add a pause at the contracted position
